title: cistus hirsutus f. pumilus
intro: Cistus hirsutus f. pumilus, also known as the small-leaved or dwarf rockrose, is a shrubby, perennial plant belonging to the rockrose family (Cistaceae). It is native to the Mediterranean region and has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Cistus hirsutus f. pumilus make it beneficial for various health issues, including wound healing, sore throat, and digestive problems, as well as offering protection against oxidative stress and inflammation. The plant can be used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in herbal medicine to harness its health benefits.
